description: Rules for reconciling intended, approved, asserted, observed, and documented project states

rules.condition_description: All five states (intended, approved, asserted, observed, documented) agree on scope, schedule, and quality for the topic
Intended state differs from approved state — scope or design was approved differently than originally intended
A party asserts completion or compliance that differs from the approved scope or specifications
Field observations or inspections contradict what a party asserts about the work
What was observed in the field does not match the formal documentation (reports, photos, logs)
Field conditions diverge from original design intent without approved changes
One or more states lack sufficient documentation to perform reconciliation
Three or more states disagree, indicating systemic misalignment requiring escalation
Some state pairs agree while others diverge — partial progress toward consistency
Insufficient data or ambiguous conditions prevent deterministic reconciliation classification
